rulings.Case Title: KEITH THOMAS VS. STARR BABCOCK (Provide Access)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 7, 2012, line 1. PLAINTIFF KEITH THOMAS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN RE: BERNARD ESCHEVERRIA JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 7, 2012, LINE 9, PETITIONER WASHINGTON SQUARE FINANCIAL Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights. Denied. The agreement provides that Mr. Etcheverria will receive $15,086.07 in exchange for the discounted present value of $201,891.48 of future payments. On its face the agreement is neither fair nor reasonable nor could it possibly be in the best interests of Mr. Etcheverria. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. GOURMET CATERING, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 7, 2012, line 4. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Writ of Attachment to issue in the amount of $8,275.96. The attorneys' fees included in the Writ were reduced by $300 since no opposition was filed and no hearing is required. The order on this motion will be signed by the Court on Thursday March 8, 2012 and mailed by the clerk's office to Plaintiff's counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANATOLY SMOLKIN VS. TOWN SCHOOL FOR BOYS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Special Motion By Defts For Order Striking Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 7, 2012, LINE 13, DEFENDANT TOWN SCHOOL FOR BOYS, MR. W. BREWSTER ELY IV (Amended) Special Motion By Defendants For Order Striking Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. Although the court recognizes tha plaintiff is currently incarcerated in San Francisco County Jail, he was properly served and could have filed an opposition and/or requested a continuance, but did neither. The moving papers are sufficient to satisfy defendants' burden that all of plaintiff's claims arise out of activities protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
